Here I will explain the process to build an object detector model using YOLOv8. In particular we will see how to use the YOLO models family to build a model that tracks in real time the ball during soccer matches.

Introduction

The

I last years a lot of models have been relased, for this project I chosed YOLO (in particular the 8-th version). The major reason behind this decision is the speed. YOLO is a one stage object detection architecture: this means that localization and classification are performed in

a single forward pass. This aspects improves speed and makes the model particularly suited for real-time detection. It can process on average more the 100 FPS (Frame-per-second) where other models like Faster R-CNN, R-CNN have a thoughput of 10 FPS.

The architecture can be split up into three main components:
1) A backbone that consists of convolutional layers for feature extraction, interleaved with a layer called C2f that is used to provide richer gradient flow information. At the end of the backbone a SPPF module used.
2) A PAN-FPN architecture is employed to
